data.title: Bitcoin Market Trends: Bearish Indicators and Investment Strategies
data.description:
Bitcoin's market trends are under scrutiny as analysts assess whether it has entered a bearish phase. According to Foresight News, a common indicator is the frequency of significant declines within a 30-day period. During bullish phases, drops exceeding 20% are rare, whereas in bearish phases, such declines occur more frequently. Market momentum has been weakening since mid-2025, with the rate of change (ROC) showing a decline in upward peaks and a reduction in buying pressure.

Experienced crypto investors understand that bear markets are a natural and recurring part of the cycle, often setting the stage for recovery and upward movement. However, bear markets do not typically decline in a straight line; substantial interim rebounds can occur even within a broader downtrend. Investors are advised to maintain discipline and continuously seek cost-effective opportunities as market conditions evolve.

data.title: Bitcoin Approaches Key Bear Market Indicator
data.description:
Bitcoin has entered a bear market level according to an analysis model by CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost. According to PANews, the model is based on the four-year simple moving average (SMA) of Bitcoin, using standard deviation and SMA multiples to assess Bitcoin's valuation. A higher multiple indicates a deeper overbought condition, while a price closer to the four-year SMA suggests a lower valuation.

Currently, Bitcoin's price has fallen into the green zone, nearing the four-year SMA level, which is approximately $57,500. Historically, this level often marks the final stage of each bear market, with BTC prices typically consolidating in this area for several months. Although Darkfost generally does not favor models based on moving averages, he believes this indicator is worth monitoring.

data.title: Hungarian Opposition Leader Begins Final Campaign Push Ahead of April Election
data.description: Hungarian opposition leader Peter Magyar has launched the final phase of his campaign for the upcoming April election, expressing confidence in his ability to end Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year tenure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Magyar's assertion that the political landscape in Hungary is poised for change. As the election approaches, Magyar's campaign efforts intensify, aiming to sway voters and challenge Orban's long-standing leadership.

data.title: AirAsia's Branding Unit Nears Nasdaq Listing Target
data.description: AirAsia founder Tony Fernandes announced that the airline's branding division is approaching a Nasdaq-listed target for a backdoor listing expected later this year. Bloomberg posted on X that this move would value the newly formed entity at approximately $1.5 billion. The strategic listing aims to enhance the company's market presence and financial standing.

data.title: A whale lost over $11.87 million on Hyperliquid, holding long positions in ETH and SOL with 20x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale (0x8d0E...9244) deposited $1 million USDC into Hyperliquid and opened a 20x leveraged long position in ETH. It currently also holds a 20x leveraged long position in SOL. Furthermore, this whale increased its spot holdings, including 20,000 SOL (worth $1.7 million), 8.5 million FARTCOIN (worth $1.69 million), 30 million MON (worth $717,000), and 6.73 million XPL (worth $651,000). Currently, this whale has suffered a total loss exceeding $11.877 million.

data.title: Japan Rejects China's Militarism Accusation
data.description:
Japan has dismissed allegations made by China's top diplomat Wang Yi, who claimed that Tokyo is reverting to militarism. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Japan's firm stance against the accusation, stating that it does not align with the current reality. The diplomatic exchange underscores ongoing tensions between the two nations, as Japan continues to refute claims that challenge its defense policies and historical context.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/292115908218210?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: STOCKS | Mixed Performance in Hong Kong Semiconductor Sector
data.description: The Hong Kong semiconductor sector experienced mixed performance recently. According to Jin10, Zhaoyi Innovation (03986.HK) saw a significant increase of 14%, while Montage Technology (06809.HK) rose by 9.19%. In contrast, SMIC (00981.HK) experienced a slight decline of 0.5%, and InnoCare Pharma (02577.HK) fell by 1.57%.

data.title: Binance Launches Word of the Day Game with HOME Token Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has introduced a new Word of the Day (WOTD) game, themed 'Account Defense,' aimed at enhancing users' crypto vocabulary and awareness of market trends. The activity period is set from 2026-02-16 00:00 (UTC) to 2026-02-22 23:59 (UTC). Participants who successfully complete three words during this period will share in 400,000 HOME token vouchers, with a maximum reward of 80 HOME per winner.

The WOTD game allows eligible users to engage in up to two games daily, testing their knowledge on the specified topic. To unlock the second game, users must share a selected article on social media, which becomes effective once a third party clicks the shared link. Additionally, new users registering with the 'WOTD' referral code during the activity period will receive a 10% discount on Spot trading fees and may qualify for further rewards by completing tasks in the Rewards Hub within 14 days of registration.

Binance emphasizes that the WOTD game may not be available in all regions, and only verified accounts from eligible areas can participate and receive rewards. All token rewards are scheduled for distribution by 2026-03-09 23:59 (UTC), and users can redeem their vouchers via the Rewards Hub. Binance retains the right to modify or cancel the promotion without prior notice and to disqualify participants involved in fraudulent activities or violations of platform terms. The platform also reserves the right to amend the activity terms at its discretion, including changes to eligibility, winner selection, and timing.

data.title: Crypto lifts PAC spend as SEC rules, 2026 midterms loom
data.description:
Data shows PAC war chests building for 2026, shaping SEC rules vs laws; Public Citizen flags disclosure gaps tied to crypto PAC spending and Citizens United.
source: https://coincu.com/news/crypto-lifts-pac-spend-as-sec-rules-2026-midterms-loom/

data.title: A whale holding $166 million worth of ETH withdrew 19,820 ETH from Binance and OKX.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale (0x28eF) withdrew 19,820 ETH, worth $40.14 million, from Binance and OKX in the past 20 hours. This address had previously purchased 60,784 ETH, worth $126 million, and is currently continuing to accumulate more.

data.title: India's Clean Energy Expansion Faces Resistance from Banks and State Power Firms
data.description: India plans to nearly double its clean energy installed capacity to 500 gigawatts by 2030. According to Jin10, this ambitious initiative is encountering opposition from banks and state-owned power companies. The resistance stems from concerns over financial viability and infrastructure challenges, which could hinder the country's transition to renewable energy sources. Despite these obstacles, India remains committed to its goal of enhancing its clean energy footprint, aiming to reduce reliance on fossil fuels and promote sustainable development.

data.title: Japan Rejects China's Militarism Accusation
data.description: Japan has dismissed allegations made by China's top diplomat Wang Yi, who claimed that Tokyo is reverting to militarism. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Japan's firm stance against the accusation, stating that it does not align with the current reality. The diplomatic exchange underscores ongoing tensions between the two nations, as Japan continues to refute claims that challenge its defense policies and historical context.

data.title: Solution worse than problem? Adam Back opposes BIP-110 Ordinals fix
data.description:
Blockstream's Adam Back said BIP-110 could be used to freeze user funds, but the proposal’s creator argues there are safeguards in place to prevent it.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/adam-back-opposes-bip110-fixing-arbitrary-data-spam?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: DefiLama founder: Projects that issue tokens have a 50% higher failure rate after achieving Product-Market Fit (PMF) than projects that don't issue tokens.
data.description: DefiLama founder 0xngmi stated in an article on the X platform that among projects that have reached the product-market fit point (TVL exceeding $10 million or monthly transaction fee revenue exceeding $1 million), projects that issue tokens have a 50% higher probability of failure than projects that do not issue tokens.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Official May Resign Amid Tensions with Secretary
data.description: A report from Jin10 indicates that U.S. Treasury Undersecretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence, John Hurley, may resign due to strained relations with Treasury Secretary Besent. According to Jin10, this potential departure would represent the latest personnel change within the upper echelons of the U.S. Treasury Department.

data.title: BNC Shareholder Update: SEC Review Underway
data.description: BNC has provided an update to its shareholders regarding recent developments. YZi Labs posted on X that a revised preliminary consent was filed with the SEC last Friday, February 13, and is currently under review. Shareholders are advised that they cannot vote or submit consents at this time. Further updates on the timing of these actions will be provided as they become available. The company expressed its commitment to strengthening BNC in collaboration with its shareholders.

data.title: Hong Kong's Potential as a Gold Trading Hub Highlighted by Chief Executive
data.description:
Hong Kong Chief Executive John Lee has identified the expansion of the gold trading center as a significant opportunity for the region's financial diversification. According to RTHK, Lee emphasized in an interview with Hong Kong Economic Journal and Now TV that while Hong Kong's stock market has seen considerable success, the increasing complexity of global politics has led investors to favor gold for its safe-haven qualities.

Lee noted that during last year's policy address consultations, it became evident that many mainland institutional investors are actively involved in gold trading. These investors not only trade domestically but also have substantial demand for international gold markets, making them key participants in the global gold market. Establishing Hong Kong as a gold trading hub in the Asia-Pacific region would facilitate physical gold delivery for mainland investors.

Currently, Hong Kong's gold trading market operates on a membership basis, which limits participation. Lee envisions a future where gold trading is as accessible as stock trading, allowing investors from various regions to participate. This would require the development of an efficient and reliable settlement system. Additionally, Lee mentioned the goal of establishing a gold storage facility in Hong Kong, aiming to surpass 2,000 tons within three years to support the future gold trading settlement system.

Lee believes that if Hong Kong becomes a gold trading center, it would lead to increased trading, settlement, and physical delivery activities in the region, creating a significant multiplier effect. This would not only boost the development of financial products but also create more opportunities for professional services, making it a win-win policy.

He also commented on the investment market, noting that while some investors are optimistic, others are pessimistic. Savvy investors will bet on the right stocks, while some may misjudge the situation. Lee suggested that some negative comments might stem from competitors' jealousy or political motives. He added that overseas investors he has interacted with in recent years have a positive view of Hong Kong, which remains the world's third-largest financial center. Last year, Hong Kong regained its position as the top global market for initial public offering fundraising.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Retail investors on the platform have been buying on dips in the current market.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in an article on the X platform that, according to Coinbase data, retail investors have been buying on dips in the current market, resulting in an increase in their holdings of BTC and ETH native units. Furthermore, the vast majority of customers had native unit balances in February equal to or greater than their balances in December of last year.

data.title: MYX Finance Token Experiences 72% Decline Amid Negative Market Sentiment
data.description: MYX Finance's token has experienced a significant 72% decline over the past week, reaching its lowest point in three months. According to NS3.AI, this downturn is primarily attributed to aggressive short selling and negative market sentiment, rather than fundamental issues or a decrease in user engagement. Despite the sharp drop, the total value locked in the platform has only seen a modest decrease. Key support levels at $1.68 and $1.43 are crucial for determining future price movements. A potential recovery could be on the horizon if the token price surpasses $2.48.

data.title: Bitcoin Cost Analysis Reveals Varied Investor Metrics
data.description: Glassnode has released data indicating different cost metrics for Bitcoin investors. According to Foresight News, short-term holders have a cost basis of $90,900, while active investors average $85,800. The true market mean stands at $79,000, with the realized price being $54,900.
